# terrible_dellemc_unity_filesystem (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `state` (String) State variable to determine whether filesystem will exist or not.

### Optional

- `access_policy` (String) Access policy of a filesystem.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `cap_unit` (String) The unit of the filesystem size. It defaults to `GB`, if not specified.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `data_reduction` (Boolean) Boolean variable, specifies whether or not to enable compression. Compression is supported only for thin filesystem.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description about the filesystem. Description can be removed by passing empty string ("").
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `filesystem_id` (String) The id of the filesystem. It can be used only for get, modify, or delete operations. It is mutually exclusive with *filesystem_name*.
- `filesystem_name` (String) The name of the filesystem. Mandatory only for the create operation. All the operations are supported through *filesystem_name*. It is mutually exclusive with *filesystem_id*.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `is_thin` (Boolean) Boolean variable, specifies whether or not it is a thin filesystem.
- `locking_policy` (String) File system locking policies. These policy choices control whether the NFSv4 range locks must be honored.
- `nas_server_id` (String) ID of the NAS server on which filesystem will be hosted.
- `nas_server_name` (String) Name of the NAS server on which filesystem will be hosted.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `pool_id` (String) This is the ID of the pool where the filesystem will be created. Either the *pool_name* or *pool_id* must be provided to create a new filesystem.
- `pool_name` (String) This is the name of the pool where the filesystem will be created. Either the *pool_name* or *pool_id* must be provided to create a new filesystem.
- `quota_config` (String) Configuration for quota management. It contains optional parameters.
- `replication_params` (String) Settings required for enabling or modifying replication.
- `replication_state` (String) State of the replication.
- `size` (Number) The size of the filesystem.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `smb_properties` (String) Advance settings for SMB. It contains optional candidate variables.
- `snap_schedule_id` (String) This is the id of an existing snapshot schedule which is to be associated with the filesystem. This is mutually exclusive with *snapshot_schedule_name*.
- `snap_schedule_name` (String) This is the name of an existing snapshot schedule which is to be associated with the filesystem. This is mutually exclusive with *snapshot_schedule_id*.
- `supported_protocols` (String) Protocols supported by the file system. It will be overridden by NAS server configuration if NAS Server is `Multiprotocol`.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tiering_policy` (String) Tiering policy choices for how the storage resource data will be distributed among the tiers available in the pool.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `filesystem_details` (String) Details of the filesystem.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
